message:
So my friend texted me at 6 PM last week in an absolute panic: 'THE POWER'S OUT IN MY NEIGHBORHOOD.' Followed by like five more increasingly frantic messages. I called him and he was genuinely spiraling—phone at 12%, no flashlight, convinced he'd be eating warm ice cream by candlelight all night.

Turns out the outage lasted two hours. But it got me thinking: most of us aren't actually *afraid* of the dark—we're just... unprepared. And then unprepared feels like panic.

I told him about keeping a couple of decent flashlights and a power bank in an easy-to-reach spot. Nothing fancy. He looked at me like I'd suggested he buy a bunker, but honestly? Just knowing "oh, I have that" takes away like 80% of the stress.

Have you got a spot where you keep your backup power stuff, or is that still on the someday list? 😄
